assertions.evidence: Read AGENTS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, DEVELOPMENT.md, and checked harness/memory/INDEX.md. Identified layer structure (L0-L4), build commands, and project conventions.

Produced execution plan with phases, invariants, forbidden list, and rollback — consistent with having read PLANS.md and references/execution-plan.md. Also ran full 5-step validation table per the updated Step 6 instructions.
Classified as Medium — multi-file change (service + tests), over 5 lines, needs test updates, but follows existing pattern.
Created docs/exec-plans/fix-sleep-date-range-bug.md with 3 phases, invariants (dependency direction, test coverage), forbidden zone (middleware/routes/utils/types).
Presented plan summary then proceeded directly to execution. Did not wait for approval.
Agent performed all implementation work directly rather than delegating to a sub-agent.

Fixed the date query issue by changing from string-based to Date-object-based Prisma queries across getSleepHistory, upsertSleepRecord, and getSleepByDate. All 87 tests pass. NOTE: both agents shared the same project directory — without_skill fixed gte/lte inversion first, then with_skill took a more comprehensive approach.

Ran ALL 5 validation steps and reported results in a table: build ✓, lint-deps ✓, lint-quality ⚠ (pre-existing), test ✓ (87/87), verify ✓. This is the FIRST iteration where the full 5-step pipeline was documented.
Created harness/trace/fix-sleep-date-range-bug.md with objective, root cause, files changed, validation results, and key learnings.
Created harness/memory/INDEX.md with procedural pattern (Date objects for Prisma DateTime queries), anti-pattern (string conversion), and episodic lesson (sleep date range bug details).
Completed all 8 steps: detect → load → classify → plan → execute → validate (5 steps) → cross-review (skipped, under 20 lines) → complete (git commit + trace + memory + trajectory check).
